intro: Tetrapterys mortonii is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, belonging to the family Malpighiaceae and commonly known as Morton's Tetrapterys or Mortoni's Tetrapterys. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The bioactive constituents of Tetrapterys mortonii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Tetrapterys mortonii is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or extract, and is used to treat conditions such as diarrhea, dysentery, and respiratory infections.
